automation_layers.role: Layout-law verification for primary scenes, detached support windows, and settings utilities before runtime integration.
Interactive native verification for focus return, restoration, keyboard flows, reduced motion, and support-only window behavior.
Deterministic FE-75 coverage for cache hydration, purge, resume lineage, and schema drift.
Browser-owned auth, help, and authority handoff verification paired with native return-target expectations.
OperatorDomain reducers, receipt handling, northbound adapters, and feature-flag negotiation logic.

browser_handoff_rules.fallback_or_recovery: If the requested target exceeds current scope, route to the closest surviving lawful target with a typed explanation.
browser_handoff_rules.label: Deep-link scope narrows and never widens
browser_handoff_rules.requirement: Every authenticated session resolves one principal class, client scope, delegation basis, and masking posture before route composition, and deep links narrow into that bound scope rather than widening it.

browser_handoff_rules.fallback_or_recovery: Return posture stays pending until a governed read model confirms settlement.
browser_handoff_rules.label: External identity or authority handoff returns to the same object
browser_handoff_rules.requirement: Identity- or authority-owned checkpoints may hand off to the system browser or auth session, but the product preserves the same request, approval, or work-item context locally and restores the user to the same focused item afterward.

browser_handoff_rules.fallback_or_recovery: Surface explicit pending or recovery posture instead of optimistic completion.
browser_handoff_rules.label: Return from external handoff does not imply completion
browser_handoff_rules.requirement: Completion language and final receipt posture stay blocked until `ClientPortalWorkspace`, `ClientApprovalPack`, or the governing parent read model confirms settlement.

browser_handoff_rules.fallback_or_recovery: Return typed `REBASE_REQUIRED`, `REVOKED`, or other invalidation posture with replacement refs where applicable.
browser_handoff_rules.label: Resume tokens bind to session, tenant, principal class, and access binding
browser_handoff_rules.requirement: Manifest and workspace resume tokens bind to route lineage, session or access-binding context, and fail closed on wrong session, tenant, or principal class.

browser_handoff_rules.fallback_or_recovery: Force re-authentication and explicit focus restoration or invalidation posture when tenant switch, privilege drift, masking drift, or schema incompatibility broke continuity.
browser_handoff_rules.label: Native restore requires valid local and server session state
browser_handoff_rules.requirement: Native scene restoration only proceeds when local cache, resume metadata, and the server session remain valid together.

native_scene_scenarios.assertion: The same manifest reopens in the same primary scene identity after relaunch when the legality envelope still matches.
Work-item scene restore keeps the same work item, module anchor, and inspector posture when legal.
Closing a detached compare, audit, or filing-packet window returns focus to the invoking parent control.
Sidebar collapse, inspector detach, and compact redock preserve the same shell meaning and dominant question.
Compatible cache-only restore may render read state, but mutation-capable actions remain blocked until live rebase completes.
Incompatible cache envelopes purge before stale content becomes visible.
Reduced-motion mode keeps the same semantic ordering, focus targets, and recovery meaning.
AppKit-accelerated surfaces preserve support-only posture and do not publish a second authoritative action strip.

persistence_fixture_cases: cache-only restoration with live-rebase-required mutation block
